results.title: Marathon Petroleum AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Marathon Petroleum Corporation has positioned itself as a leader in digital refinery transformation, leveraging AI technologies for operational excellence and future-proofing its business model[1][2]. The company has deployed AI across multiple operational functions, including predictive maintenance, refinery optimization, and coker cycle management, with notable successes such as a 25% reduction in suboptimal drum cycles in their coke drum operations[3]. Marathon has also embraced AI-driven partnerships and strategic initiatives, including a collaboration with MPLX and MARA Holdings to power AI data centers with natural gas, representing a strategic pivot from internal AI optimization to becoming an external enabler of the AI ecosystem[4][5].

What AI technologies is Marathon Petroleum using in its refineries?
...
Marathon uses C3 AI Reliability for predictive maintenance across 16 refineries, Imubit for closed-loop process optimization, Dynamic Risk Analyzer for anomaly detection, and a proprietary large language model for technical standards verification.
...
Through its subsidiary MPLX, Marathon has partnered with MARA Holdings to provide natural gas for AI data center power generation in West Texas, with a 1.5-gigawatt power deal to support high-performance computing and AI workloads.
...
has achieved a 25% reduction in suboptimal drum cycles in coker operations,

with vendors?
...
Marathon uses a hybrid approach, partnering with AI vendors like C3 AI and Imubit while also developing internal capabilities such as a proprietary large language model for technical standards.
...
AI enables predictive maintenance to prevent equipment failures before they occur, automates data analysis across thousands of process signals, and helps transition from reactive to predictive maintenance strategies, reducing downtime and costs.

results.title: Ch Sam
highlights:
Software Engineer at [Marathon Petroleum Corporation](https://www.linkedin.com/company/marathon-petroleum-company)
...
Delivered latency-sensitive, high-throughput APIs and production applications in Java, Python, TypeScript, Node.js, and React, RESTful and event-driven at scale → DevOps and Reliability: CI/CD pipelines, DevSecOps practices, containerized microservices with Docker and Kubernetes, and serverless architectures built for zero
...
owntime production environments → Generative AI and Agentic Systems: Production RAG pipelines, LLM integration, agentic workflows, and MLOps tooling built with the same engineering rigor I bring to any production system. I write clean, testable code, own systems end-to-end, and communicate architecture clearly across technical and non-technical stakeholders. I've worked as a high-ownership IC and in cross-functional Agile teams. I adapt to the environment and focus on shipping things that matter. Currently
...
### Software Engineer at [Marathon Petroleum Corporation](https://www.linkedin.com/company/marathon-petroleum-company) (Current)
...
At Marathon, I work as a Software Engineer building and deploying production-grade AI/ML systems that power core business operations. My role sits at the intersection of applied AI and backend engineering — designing systems that are reliable, maintainable, and built to perform under real-world demand. On the AI/ML side, I design and deploy production ML systems on AWS SageMaker — spanning feature engineering, model training, and real-time inference endpoints. I built RAG pipelines using LangChain and vector databases that enable natural language querying of internal knowledge bases, significantly reducing analyst turnaround time. I have also integrated LLM-based automation into operational workflows, enabling intelligent anomaly detection and alerting at scale. On the engineering side, I build and own the full backend infrastructure that supports these AI systems. REST APIs are built with FastAPI and Node.js, deployed serverlessly on AWS Lambda behind API Gateway. Infrastructure is fully provisioned with Terraform, containerized workloads run on Kubernetes via Amazon EKS, and CI/CD pipelines are automated end-to-end through GitHub Actions — covering build, test, security scan, and deployment stages. I also build React-based dashboards that surface ML insights directly to business stakeholders. I own the architecture of the systems I build — from whiteboard to production — and collaborate closely with data scientists, platform engineers, and business stakeholders to deliver solutions that create measurable impact. Core stack: Python
...
- AWS (SageMaker, Lambda, API Gateway, EKS)
...
- LangChain
...
- RAG
...
- LLMs
...
- Agentic AI
...
. The backend is a TypeScript/Node.js microservice deployed on AWS Lambda behind API Gateway, designed for cold-start performance and horizontal scalability. Conversation context is persisted in DynamoDB, enabling multi-turn dialogue with memory. The frontend is built in React with streaming response rendering — responses appear token by token, matching the feel of modern AI chat interfaces. All infrastructure is Terraform-provisioned on AWS. GitHub Actions handles the full CI/CD pipeline — including automated prompt regression testing, embedding drift detection, and model evaluation gates before every production deployment. CloudWatch and custom dashboards in Grafana provide observability across retrieval quality, latency, and cost per query. MLOps practices are baked in: prompt versions are tracked, model outputs are evaluated automatically against a golden dataset on each deployment, and retrieval relevance scores are monitored in production. The system reduced internal knowledge-search time by an estimated 65% in early team trials and is actively used to onboard new engineers faster. Show less
...
⚡ Generative AI in production — RAG pipelines, agentic AI systems, LLM integration, MLOps

results.title: Akshith MA
highlights:
Data Engineer at [Marathon Petroleum Corporation](https://www.linkedin.com/company/marathon-petroleum-company)
...
### Data Engineer at [Marathon Petroleum Corporation](https://www.linkedin.com/company/marathon-petroleum-company) (Current)
...
Designed and deployed scalable, secure data ingestion pipelines using Azure Data Factory, integrating sources like SQL, REST APIs, and cloud storage. Built and optimized high-performance Azure Databricks workflows for distributed data transformation using Spark, PySpark, and Delta Lake. Leveraged Azure Synapse Analytics for large-scale data processing and analysis, enhancing query performance and system efficiency. Implemented near real-time data streaming using Apache Spark Structured Streaming, Kafka, and Azure Event Hubs to support analytics on live data. Ensured robust data validation, cleansing, and transformation for improved data quality and integrity. Used Azure Key Vault and Azure Function Apps to secure access and automate workflows. Developed data pipelines for loading JSON into SQL databases, cutting data processing time by 50%. Managed metadata and data lineage across pipelines for governance and traceability. Created optimized Snowflake schemas, leveraging features like partitioning, indexing, and caching for faster data retrieval. Built custom monitoring dashboards with Azure Monitor and Snowflake Query Performance Monitoring, proactively addressing bottlenecks. Interfaced with external APIs to integrate JSON-based data into enterprise systems. Applied Python for data quality checks, automation, and validation logic across data pipelines. Collaborated cross-functionally with analysts, scientists, and business teams to align data solutions with strategic goals. Actively engaged in Agile development with participation in daily stand-ups and globally coordinated PI planning.
...
Spearheaded the development of scalable, end-to-end data pipelines using Azure Data Factory and Snowflake, streamlining Marathon Petroleum's data transformation processes across disparate sources. Enabled real-time analytics by designing event-driven architectures with Azure Event Hubs and Functions, significantly improving Marathon's ability to act on high-velocity operational data. Architected and implemented Azure Databricks-based data workflows, utilizing Spark to power complex ETL operations and large-scale analytics for Marathon’s logistics and fuel supply chain data. Optimized big data storage using Azure Data Lake and Azure Synapse with PolyBase, reducing data retrieval time by 40% and supporting near real-time reporting requirements. Enhanced data security and compliance for Marathon by applying robust encryption and retention policies via Azure Blob Storage and Snowflake's Time Travel feature. Integrated Azure Logic Apps with data pipelines to enable business rule-based automation and real-time orchestration across logistics systems and third-party vendors. Deployed machine learning workflows with Azure ML and Snowflake, empowering predictive analytics for fleet maintenance and fuel demand forecasting. Established a unified data catalog and lineage using Azure Purview and Apache Atlas, increasing data traceability and audit readiness. Improved cross-team visibility and performance tracking by integrating Snowflake with Power BI and Azure Analysis Services, enabling interactive dashboards for Marathon’s stakeholders. Automated CI/CD for data pipelines using Python and Azure DevOps, cutting release cycles and ensuring consistent deployment to production environments.

### Senior Software Engineer at [Marathon Petroleum Corporation](https://www.linkedin.com/company/marathon-petroleum-company) (Current)

Senior Software Engineer for Finance IT at Marathon Petroleum Corporation. Sreekanth serves as SAP technical lead for Marathon's IT-Finance, supporting SAP S/4 HANA 2022. I oversee the daily technical support of SAP Finance modules RTR, PTP, and MDG. Additionally, I spearhead the modernization of Marathon's SAP systems, including migrating SAP S/4 HANA 2022 to the cloud (SAP RISE) and enhancing the systems with clean core and new BTP services to provide additional functionality to end users. The job duties also include “build and support” for reporting functionality and for system maintenance using SAP development technologies including S/4 HANA (Release 1610 and Release 1909) and SAP ERP Central Component 6.0 (“ECC 6.0”). Responsible for end-to-end design, development, implementation, enhancement, and maintenance for complex SAP applications. Responsible for leading the technical team for support of SAP systems in the Finance (FI) area. Coordinates with business users and other IT departments to understand business processes and to gather requirements to design developments, to provide critical SAP solutions, to implement and maintain SAP applications using various SAP development components—including S/4 HANA, ABAP, SAP HANA, ABAP for HANA, AIF, UI5, Fiori Applications, Adobe Forms, SAP WAS, BSP, SAP Enterprise Portal, SAP Webdynpro, Classical and Interactive Reports, Module Pool, Workflow, BADIs, Enhancements (User Exits), BAPI, BDCs, LSMW, OOPS, RFC, IDOCs, and SOLMAN and adheres to the Company’s internal development standards, guidelines, and best practices.

highlights:
Senior Crude Oil Scheduler at [Marathon Petroleum Corporation](https://www.linkedin.com/company/marathon-petroleum-company)
...
I am an experienced logistics and data analyst with a background in oil and gas. I started my career as an analyst and after a few years in the industry, I pivoted into a marketing/commercial role. I am currently enrolled in an intensive Data Science bootcamp at Springboard where I am developing my expertise in the Data Science process, Python, SQL, Machine Learning, Statistics, GitHub, Data Analysis and Modeling, and other useful tools/skills. I would love the opportunity to combine my 10 years of commercial and analytical knowledge from the hydrocarbon industry, with newfound Data Science skills at a forward thinking energy company. I love the idea of meshing energy from hydrocarbons with green energy and renewables to help guide our energy usage in the future. Through my career I have always been exposed to data, and a need to manipulate data. My new skills with the Data Science process will help me and future companies I work for understand their data and use it in an effective way to drive business results. On a personal note, I grew up racing mountain bikes, I learned a lot about hard work, dedication, teamwork, and personal connections/networking. Those qualities have helped me throughout my career and will continue to help me in the future.

The ideal candidate will have a strong background in machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making. The role requires collaboration with cross-functional teams to design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems.

Python AWS Azure GCP Databricks LangChain LangGraph LlamaIndex Hugging Face AutoGen
...
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for an AI / ML Engineer in the United States.This role sits at the intersection of machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making, focused on solving complex challenges in the energy and utility sector. You will contribute to the development of advanced AI and ML solutions that combine predictive modeling, optimization, and generative AI to support critical infrastructure and sustainability goals. Working closely with data scientists and software engineers, you will help design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems that power real-world applications. The environment is highly collaborative and innovation-driven, with a strong emphasis on applied AI, production readiness, and measurable impact. You will work across diverse use cases including forecasting, geospatial analysis, and intelligent decision systems. This is a high-impact role where your work directly supports the modernization and efficiency of energy systems.Accountabilities
...

...

and monitoring AI

, including reasoning

, and fairness.

...

...

Data Scientist (100%) - MDPI Switzerland
...
Product & Technology · Basel Office - MDPI Headquarters
...
# Data Scientist (100%)
...
## Drive innovation in machine learning and AI, from research to deployment, creating scalable intelligent systems that power the next generation of scientific discovery.
...
Are you passionate about turning complex data into intelligent, production-ready solutions while supporting the future of open-access science? We are looking for a Data Scientist to join our team and help design, develop, and deploy advanced machine learning solutions that power intelligent, data-driven products. This role focuses on NLP, recommender systems, and agentic LLM workflows, turning complex business challenges into scalable and impactful analytical solutions.
...
This is an opportunity to work at the forefront of machine learning and AI, contributing to innovative systems that leverage state-of-the-art research in practical applications.
...
Core Responsibilities
...
Design, develop, and evaluate machine learning and statistical models, with a focus on NLP, recommender systems, and LLM-based solutions.
...
Translate business problems into data-driven approaches and scalable analytical solutions.
...
Perform data exploration, preprocessing, and feature engineering to ensure high-quality inputs for modeling.
...
Develop, optimize, and benchmark models using appropriate metrics and validation strategies.
...
Build and experiment with LLM-powered systems, including agentic workflows and RAG.
...
Investigate, reproduce, and compare research prototypes and state-of-the-art methods.
...
Collaborate with engineering teams to support the integration of models into production.
...
Monitor and analyze model performance and iterate to improve accuracy and robustness.
...
Bachelor’s degree/ Master's degree in Computer Science or related.
...
2-5 years of experience as a Data Scientist.
...
2–5 years of experience in Python (including complex applications), Machine Learning, and LLMs.
...
Strong background in data science, statistics, and analytical problem-solving.
...
Intermediate proficiency in FastAPI, Celery, and Keycloak.
...
Intermediate proficiency in PyTorch, TensorFlow, Scikit-learn, and Hugging Face.
...
Proficiency in Natural Language Processing (NLP), including tokenization and named entity recognition (NER).
...
In-depth understanding of Artificial Intelligence principles.
...
Strong working knowledge of Microsoft O365 tools.
